REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT OR TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE; PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. THE PARTIES APPEAR TO BE IN AGREEMENT AS TO THE SALE OF THE 30TH AVENUE PROPERTY AND SHALL PROCEED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THAT AGREEMENT. THE PROVISIONS OF THE AGREEMENT REGARDING THE 22ND AVENUE PROPERTY ARE UNAMBIGUOUS IN THAT ONLY "IN THE EVENT THAT DEFENDANTS DECIDE TO SELL THE 22ND AVENUE PROPERTY, THE PARTIES AGREE TO USE THE REALTOR SELECTED TO HANDLE THE SALE OF THE 30TH AVENUE PROPERTY." THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS DECIDED TO SELL THE PROPERTY. THERE IS NO AMBIGUITY IN THE AGREEMENT. THE PARTIES MAY PROCEED WITH THE PROBATE COURT PROCEDURES TO DETERMINE IF THE OFFERED BUY-OUT IS CONFIRMED. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR SET ASIDE A FULLY EXECUTED UNAMBIGUOUS BINDING AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE COURT WILL NOT ENTERTAIN DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO ENTER JUDGMENT AS IT IS IMPROPERLY MADE IN OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. ANY SUCH REQUEST SHOULD BE MADE, IF NECESSARY AND PROPER, IN A SEPARATE PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, PHIL FOSTER IS TO TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JENNY SHU CHANG, STELLA SHU LOO, PHILLIP SHU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF SETTLEMENT IS DENIED AS REQUESTED. PLAINTIFF MOVES FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR AND AGAINST THE DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF POINTS TO NO PROVISION IN THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT THAT WOULD SUPPORT AN ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN SUCH FORM. WHILE PLAINTIFF CITES TO HINES (IN REPLY) PLAINTIFF IS NOT SEEKING "TO ENTER A JUDGMENT REFLECTING ALL OF THE MATERIAL TERMS OF SETTLEMENT THAT HAVE YET TO BE FULLY PERFORMED." NOTHING IN CCP 664.6 COMPELS ENTRY OF THE JUDGMENT IN THE FORM REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF IN ABSENCE OF EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF IS IN FACT ENTITLED TO A JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR PURSUANT TO SUCH AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND ARGUMENT RAISED IN REPLY ALSO DOES NOT WARRANT SUCH RESULT. CCP 664.6 ALLOWS THE COURT TO RETAIN JURISDICTION PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, A SIMPLE REQUEST TO THE COURT TO DO SO WOULD ACCOMPLISH THE GOAL, WHILE AN ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF WOULD BE CONTRARY TO THE PARTIES' EXPRESS AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendants Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-02-2021 Continued To Feb-03-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Phil Foster; Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP; 415-626-7744; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Jenny Sai-Yun Shu Chang, Stella Sai-Fin Shu Loo, and Phillip Shu, et al.
Donel R. Lopez; Lepera + Associates, PC; 415-362-2529; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendants Sara Shu Chan and Esther Shu.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Continued To Mar-02-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances vis Court Call:
Phil Foster, Esq., (415) 626-7744, appearing for Jenny Sai-Yun Shu Chang,, Plaintiff
Donel Lopez, Esq., (415) 362-2529, appearing for Sara Shu Chan and Esther
Shu, Defendant

ARGUED. Mr. Foster stated that he contested the tentative ruling, provided case background and argued about the type of judgment that he would like. Mr. Lopez provided a status update on the sale of the real property, addressed the Wells Fargo account and provided further case background.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENNY SHU CHANG, STELLA SHU LOO, PHILLIP SHU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement is DENIED as requested. Plaintiff moves for entry of judgment in its favor and against the defendant. Plaintiff points to no provision in the settlement agreement that would support an entry of judgment in such form. While Plaintiff cites to Hines (in reply) Plaintiff is not seeking "to enter a judgment reflecting all of the material terms of settlement that have yet to be fully performed." Nothing in CCP 664.6 compels entry of the judgment in the form requested by Plaintiff in absence of evidence that Plaintiff is in fact entitled to a judgment in its favor pursuant to such agreement. Plaintiff's second argument raised in reply also does not warrant such result. CCP 664.6 allows the Court to retain jurisdiction per agreement of the parties, a simple request to the Court to do so would accomplish the goal, while an entry of judgment in favor of Plaintiff would be contrary to the parties' express agreement. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-20-2020 Continued To Jan-05-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement Or Alternatively Setting Aside Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Phil Foster, Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices LLP, (415) 626-7744, for Plaintiff, Jenny Sai-Shu Chang, via CourtCall.
Donel Lopez, (415) 626-7744, Specially appearing for Defendant(s) Sara Shu and Esther Shu, via CourtCall.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement or to Set Aside Settlement is DENIED. Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement is Denied without prejudice; Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside Settlement is DENIED. The parties appear to be in agreement as to the sale of the 30th Avenue property and shall proceed in accordance with that agreement. The provisions of the agreement regarding the 22nd Avenue property are unambiguous in that only "in the event that Defendants decide to sell the 22nd Avenue Property, the parties agree to use the realtor selected to handle the sale of the 30th Avenue Property." There is no evidence that Defendants decided to sell the property. There is no ambiguity in the agreement. The parties may proceed with the Probate Court procedures to determine if the offered buy-out is confirmed. There is no basis for set aside a fully executed unambiguous binding agreement between the parties. The Court will not entertain Defendant's request to enter judgment as it is improperly made in opposition to Plaintiff's motion. Any such request should be made, if necessary and proper, in a separate properly noticed motion. Plaintiff's counsel, Phil Foster is to to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported (D501)
